title: WindSurf IDE
html:
WindSurf IDE

The AI-assisted IDE Yahir picked as his frontend dev environment for BroMenu / Brotein starting in late 2024 — and, alongside Claude, one of the two tools that made solo founder builds production-grade native apps viable. The IDE matters in the corpus less for what it is and more for what it enables: it is the executable answer to the November 2021 question Yahir asked himself in 2021 11 05 ceos of the future — "Do you think CEOs of the future will be at a disadvantage if they are not engineers or don't have an understanding of technology?" WindSurf is part of the toolchain that lets a non-engineer-trained CEO build native iOS without a team.

The first explicit appearance is in 2024 12 21 todos — the BroMenu spec from late December 2024, written right after departing China. The tech stack is documented cleanly: "Frontend: SwiftUI in WindSurf IDE, tested via Xcode on a physical iPhone (explicitly to avoid the Apple Developer fee). AI: OpenAI Camera API for OCR, GPT API for anabolic scoring. Backend: Supabase for storage + 'Sign in with Apple' auth. UI inspiration: AliPay / WeChat QR scan flows." The detail that matters: physical iPhone testing to avoid the Apple Developer fee — the no runway discipline showing up at the tools-and-environment level, not just the lifestyle level. Yahir picks WindSurf in part because it lets him build without the Xcode-cloud paid tier.

The 2021 2021 11 05 ceos of the future foreshadowing is the conceptual anchor: Yahir was already imagining the engineer-CEO advantage three years before he had the tools to embody it. WindSurf + Claude + OpenAI API is the toolchain that closed that gap. By 2026 he is writing strategy notes (2026 01 13 rankett com) and shipping product code in parallel — the IDE is the bridge.

The implicit graph-position: WindSurf joins Supabase, OpenAI API, Claude, ChatGPT, and GitHub Copilot (mentioned in 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream) in the build-partner cluster — the tools that make Yahir's solo-founder posture viable. Each tool is a labor-substitution: WindSurf substitutes for a senior frontend engineer's pair-programming, Claude substitutes for a thinking partner, Supabase substitutes for backend infra ops. The whole stack is the no runway discipline philosophy made operational.
